items.title: STADLER reshapes knowledge work at a 230-year-old company
items.summary: Learn how STADLER uses ChatGPT to transform knowledge work, saving time and accelerating productivity across 650 employees.
items.content: Learn how STADLER uses ChatGPT to transform knowledge work, saving time and accelerating productivity across 650 employees.

items.title: Bluesky leans into AI with Attie, an app for building custom feeds
items.summary: Bluesky’s new app Attie uses AI to help people build custom feeds the open social networking protocol atproto.
items.content: Bluesky’s new app Attie uses AI to help people build custom feeds the open social networking protocol atproto.

items.title: Stanford study outlines dangers of asking AI chatbots for personal advice
items.summary: While there’s been plenty of debate about AI sycophancy, a new study by Stanford computer scientists attempts to measure how harmful that tendency might be.
items.content: While there’s been plenty of debate about AI sycophancy, a new study by Stanford computer scientists attempts to measure how harmful that tendency might be.

items.title: Elon Musk’s last co-founder reportedly leaves xAI
items.summary: All but two of Musk's 11 xAI co-founders departed before this week.
items.content: All but two of Musk's 11 xAI co-founders departed before this week.

items.title: Anthropic’s Claude popularity with paying consumers is skyrocketing
items.summary: Estimates for total Claude consumer users are all over the map (we've seen figures ranging from 18 million to 30 million). Anthropic hasn't disclosed this data, but a spokesperson did tell TechCrunch that Claude paid subscriptions have more than doubled this year.
items.content: Estimates for total Claude consumer users are all over the map (we've seen figures ranging from 18 million to 30 million). Anthropic hasn't disclosed this data, but a spokesperson did tell TechCrunch that Claude paid subscriptions have more than doubled this year.

items.title: Why SoftBank’s new $40B loan points to a 2026 OpenAI IPO
items.summary: Wall Street giants JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs are extending a 12-month, unsecured loan to the Japanese conglomerate.
items.content: Wall Street giants JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs are extending a 12-month, unsecured loan to the Japanese conglomerate.

items.title: Memory chip giant SK hynix could help end ‘RAMmageddon’ with blockbuster US IPO
items.summary: SK hynix’s potential U.S. listing could raise $10-$14 billion to help it build more capacity, encourage others to follow, and end the 'RAMmageddon' memory shortage.
items.content: SK hynix’s potential U.S. listing could raise $10-$14 billion to help it build more capacity, encourage others to follow, and end the 'RAMmageddon' memory shortage.

items.title: VCs are betting billions on AI’s next wave, so why is OpenAI killing Sora?
items.summary: When an 82-year-old Kentucky woman was offered $26 million from an AI company that wanted to build a data center on her land, she said no. Sure, that same company can try to rezone 2,000 acres nearby anyway, but as AI infrastructure stretches further into the real world, the real world is starting to push back.  That tension is everywhere […]
